findings.title: _oos_verdict ignores benchmark_available (M46): an uncomputable buy-and-hold silently degrades the D2 excess-Sharpe PASS to a weaker absolute-Sharpe PASS
findings.detail: loop.py:572 `excess_sharpe = float(m.get("sharpe_annual",0.0)) - float(m.get("buy_hold_sharpe") or 0.0)`. When the benchmark is not computable, executor.py:205 coalesces `buy_hold_sharpe` to the float 0.0, and executor.py:207-210 sets `benchmark_available=False` precisely so downstream honesty checks 'must key on this flag, not on 0.0' (M46). `_oos_verdict` receives `benchmark_available` in `m` but never reads it. The `.get(...) or 0.0` cannot distinguish 'no benchmark' from 'benchmark with Sharpe 0', so `excess_sharpe` collapses to `sharpe_annual`. Because the PASS is `a.validates AND excess_sharpe>0` and `validates` already requires `sharpe_annual>0` (confidence.py:146/154, fed observed_sharpe=sharpe_annual at loop.py:561), the excess arm becomes a no-op and the OOS PASS is issued on absolute-Sharpe-positivity alone — a strictly weaker criterion than D2 requires ('excess Sharpe over buy-and-hold', FB3 safety doc:221/103, plan D2:14) — with no flag and no UNEVALUATED. The code comment (loop.py:570-571) documents this as intentional ('degrades to absolute risk-adjusted skill'), but that degradation is NOT sanctioned by the spec: the reconciled plan's honesty posture is UNEVALUATED / report-when-you-can't-judge, and M46 was built specifically to prevent 0.0-coalescing from masquerading as a real benchmark. Correct behavior: when `benchmark_available` is False, return UNEVALUATED (retryable, no write-once budget burned) or at minimum flag the verdict as benchmark-degraded — not silently PASS. Reachability is low in practice (benchmark_available=False needs <=2 equity bars, which typically won't reach the per_trade basis that this code path requires, so it usually returns UNEVALUATED at loop.py:565 first), but the fix is one line and the semantics are wrong wherever the state is reached. Flagging before commit.

findings.title: 'excess Sharpe' is implemented as a naive difference of annualized Sharpes, not IR/alpha — it does not beta-adjust, so it only partially delivers the market-neutral-credit motivation the change cites
findings.detail: loop.py:572 computes `sharpe_annual - buy_hold_sharpe`. D2 (plan:14) and FB3 (safety doc:221) list the risk-adjusted quantity as 'excess Sharpe / information ratio / alpha'. A raw difference of two scale-invariant Sharpe ratios is none of IR (mean(strat-bench)/std(strat-bench)) nor alpha (regression intercept); it ignores the strategy/benchmark correlation and beta. Consequence: a genuinely low-beta / market-neutral edge in a strong bull OOS window is still charged the FULL buy-and-hold Sharpe and can be failed for a negative Sharpe-difference even though its alpha is positive — exactly the beta-contamination the new test `test_market_neutral_skill_passes_despite_lower_total_return` claims to fix on the total-return axis but which the Sharpe-difference axis only partially addresses. This is within the letter of the spec (D2 explicitly permits 'excess Sharpe'), so it is a design note, not a contract break: it is a strict improvement over the deleted total-return beta bar and is symmetric/defensible, but if the intent is to certify skill isolated from beta, a regression alpha / IR is the faithful quantity. No code change required to satisfy the locked spec; recording so the choice is a conscious one.

result.summary: The change faithfully implements D2's core intent and I found no blocker. Verified defenses against the three probed attacks: (a) the 'excess vs a negative benchmark makes the bar easier than absolute' attack FAILS — the PASS is `a.validates AND excess_sharpe>0`, and `validates` independently requires `sharpe_annual>0` (confidence.py:146/154, observed_sharpe=sharpe_annual at loop.py:561), so the combined bar is `sharpe_annual>0 AND sharpe_annual>buy_hold_sharpe`, strictly harder than absolute; a negative-Sharpe strategy can never pass. (b) the fee-net formula `(1+r)(1-c)^2-1` is correct: backtesting.py commission is a per-transaction fraction of trade value, a B&H round-trip is entry+exit = 2 charges, strategy total_return is already net while buy_hold_return is gross, and all quantities are fractions (runner.py:411/416) — so squaring and the units are right (and it is report-only by default anyway). (c) Sharpe comparability holds: strategy sharpe_annual and buy_hold_sharpe are both computed over the warmup-trimmed window (runner.py:300, executor.py:185) with the same interval-aware estimator (buy_hold.py:55-59). The gatekeeper DSR-stays-HARD fix and its test correctly match plan §2c and the safety doc, and the new 3-tuple contract is consistently threaded through both production callers (loop.py:688 and _record_oos). Two findings remain: HIGH — `_oos_verdict` ignores the M46 `benchmark_available` flag, so an uncomputable benchmark silently degrades the D2 excess-Sharpe PASS to a weaker absolute-Sharpe PASS (one-line fix: route to UNEVALUATED/flag; low reachability today but wrong semantics where reached); LOW — 'excess Sharpe' is a naive Sharpe difference, not beta-adjusted IR/alpha, so the market-neutral motivation is only partly served (within the letter of D2, recorded as a conscious-choice note).

findings.title: benchmark_available flag is ignored in _oos_verdict; no-benchmark path silently degrades the risk-adjusted bar to 'absolute Sharpe > 0'
findings.detail: loop.py:572 computes excess_sharpe = sharpe_annual - (buy_hold_sharpe or 0.0) and never consults m['benchmark_available'] (emitted by executor.py:210). When the benchmark is uncomputable, buy_hold_sharpe stays 0.0, so the PASS bar becomes 'validates AND absolute sharpe_annual > 0' rather than an excess-over-benchmark test. The inline comment acknowledges this ('degrades to absolute risk-adjusted skill'). This is NOT a practical loosening: benchmark_available = bool(len(returns) > 1) is False only for a degenerate <=1-bar OOS window, where a per_trade significance test (a.validates) is essentially unreachable, so the branch is effectively dead in production; and it mirrors the old total-return arm's degradation (buy_hold_return->0.0) and the upstream gatekeeper default (gatekeeper.py:147 defaults buy_hold_sharpe to 0.0). Same defensive-FAIL note: a NaN buy_hold_sharpe (zero-variance benchmark) makes excess_sharpe NaN -> FAIL, which is conservative (tightening), not a loosening. Flagged only for completeness; no action required before commit.

result.summary: The diff is spec-faithful and safety-direction-correct; I found NO blocker or high issues. (a) Flip analysis: the only FAIL->PASS flips are validates AND excess_sharpe>0 AND total_return<=buy_hold_return — exactly D2's intended market-neutral corner; by construction every new PASS satisfies excess_sharpe>0, so there is no accidental widening. The 'validates-but-excess_sharpe<=0' corner now FAILs (tightening) and 'excess>0-but-insignificant' is unchanged (gated by a.validates). Strategy Sharpe is fee-net vs benchmark gross Sharpe, so the bar is if anything harder. (b) FB3 firewall intact: traced all consumers of state.oos_results (state.py:301 validated-set, router.py:720/852 display dicts, report_generator/persistence, and the regime /candidates sort key router.py:705-710) — none read excess_sharpe/excess_total_return_net; they are display-only in OOSResult and the emit stream, cannot steer selection/mutation/sampling. (c) DSR staying HARD in regime matches reconciled-plan §2c + OD1 option 3 + RT2 (soft-DSR sequencing-blocked until FB4); nothing in regime/idea-surfacing depends on soft DSR, and the D2 lockbox change touches only the robustness OOS flow, so the two changes don't interact. (d) Write-once/terminal semantics unchanged: lockbox.evaluate path is untouched; extras are captured in the closure and recorded post-evaluate. The fee-net total-return comparison is genuinely wired (executor.py:219 supplies 'commission') and correctly gated only by OOS_TOTAL_RETURN_FLOOR (default False = report-only). 69 relevant tests pass. One low/informational note below.

findings.title: Excess-Sharpe-over-buy-and-hold is not beta-neutral; still rejects genuine market-neutral skill in a high-Sharpe bull
findings.detail: loop.py:572 sets the PASS quantity to excess_sharpe = sharpe_annual - buy_hold_sharpe, and the PASS requires excess_sharpe > 0 (loop.py:573). This removes the total-RETURN dominance arm (the OD7 fix) but the replacement is still benchmarked against buy-and-hold's Sharpe, which is not beta-neutral. Concrete failure: a low-volatility grind-up bull (e.g. 2017-style) where compute_buy_hold yields buy_hold_sharpe ~2.0; a genuine market-neutral strategy with sharpe_annual ~1.0 that clears the per-trade significance test (a.validates) gets excess_sharpe = -1.0 -> FAIL. That is exactly the outcome OD7/FB3 says risk-adjusting is meant to prevent ('risk-adjusting the PASS quantity stops a bull-market beta bar from silently rejecting genuine market-neutral skill', COVERAGE-GATE-SAFETY-MECHANISMS.md FB3/OD7). The truly beta-neutral quantities OD7 enumerates are IR/alpha (regression intercept) or absolute Sharpe; excess-vs-B&H-Sharpe is an intermediate that is still partly a beta bar. NOTE: the spec (D2, OD7 option 2) literally lists 'excess Sharpe / IR / alpha' as acceptable, so the implementation matches one sanctioned reading -- this is an owner interpretation call, not a spec violation. Flagging because the code + tests (test_market_neutral_skill_passes_despite_lower_total_return uses buy_hold_sharpe=0.6, a LOW benchmark Sharpe) do not exercise the high-Sharpe-bull case where the chosen quantity still fails market-neutral skill, so the stated OD7 goal is only partially met.

findings.title: New D2 evidence fields (excess_sharpe / excess_total_return_net) are recorded + emitted but never surfaced in the report or /candidates dossier, contradicting the 'for the report' docstring
findings.detail: The loop.py docstring (line 542) and state.py comment (line 161-163) claim the extras are 'for the OOSResult/report -- display evidence' / 'recorded here so the verdict is auditable'. They ARE appended to state.oos_results (loop.py:611-613) and pushed onto the live activity event (loop.py:624-626, persisted into ResearchEventDB.detail_json). But no formal consumer reads them: report_generator.py reads only r.outcome (lines 71/212/262/282); router._oos_dict (router.py:727-734) and load_candidate_detail (router.py:864-867) build the candidate OOS chip from outcome/tier/ci/in_market fields only and omit excess_sharpe/excess_total_return_net; persistence stores only oos_outcome per candidate. Net effect: the D2 total-return comparison the design calls 'report-only' is visible only as raw activity-stream JSON, not in the generated report or the candidate dossier the docstring points at. Not a contract break (nothing errors; fields are additive and defaulted), but the 'report-only surfacing' is unwired -- either wire report_generator/router to show them or soften the docstring claim.

findings.title: _oos_verdict ignores benchmark_available; silently degrades excess Sharpe to ABSOLUTE Sharpe when the benchmark is uncomputable
findings.detail: loop.py:572 uses float(m.get('buy_hold_sharpe') or 0.0). When the OOS window benchmark is uncomputable, the executor sets buy_hold_sharpe = 0.0 and benchmark_available = False (executor.py:179,205,210), but _oos_verdict does not consult benchmark_available. So excess_sharpe collapses to sharpe_annual and a PASS can be granted on ABSOLUTE risk-adjusted skill with zero benchmark comparison. This is documented/intended (loop.py:570-571 comment) and matches the OLD behavior (old code degraded excess=total_return-0), and buy_hold_sharpe is guarded to 0.0 rather than NaN (metrics.py:207-211, benchmark_sharpe returns 0.0 for <3 bars / zero variance), so there is no NaN-propagation FAIL and no regression. On a real OOS window (many bars) benchmark_available is effectively always True, so the path is largely unreachable in production. Flagging only so the owner is aware the 'excess' PASS silently becomes an 'absolute' PASS on the degenerate path rather than degrading to UNEVALUATED.

result.summary: The diff is contract/integration-clean and spec-compliant. Verified: the only non-test caller (loop.py:688) unpacks the new 3-tuple; every OOSResult construction site uses defaulted keyword/positional args (test_director.py etc. pass 3 positional, unaffected); the recover path (loop.py:661, no extras), the UNEVALUATED path (returns {}), and the infra-failure/budget short-circuit paths all reach _record_oos with extras=None or {}, robustly handled by (extras or {}).get(...); OOSResult is never persisted as a whole dataclass (only .outcome round-trips via oos_map), so old persisted states load cleanly and there is no schema-migration/round-trip issue; the emit event only ADDS keys to free-form detail_json (no pydantic model, no strict TS type consuming oos_result), so no schema-strictness break. The gatekeeper DSR-stays-HARD change is correctly wired (dsr still constructed + added to the pipeline, only removed from the SOFT loop) and its test is updated; the fee-net total-return floor is off by default (report-only) and the excess-Sharpe risk-adjusted PASS matches D2/FB3/OD7. All 38 targeted/related tests pass. No blockers and no highs found. Three lower-severity findings: (1) excess-Sharpe-over-B&H is not fully beta-neutral and can still fail genuine market-neutral skill in a high-Sharpe bull (spec sanctions the quantity, so it is an owner interpretation call, and the tests only cover the low-benchmark-Sharpe case); (2) the new D2 evidence fields are recorded + emitted but not surfaced by report_generator or the /candidates dossier despite the docstring claiming they are 'for the report'; (3) benchmark_available is not consulted, so the excess PASS silently degrades to an absolute-Sharpe PASS on the (practically unreachable) uncomputable-benchmark OOS path.
